intro: Oncorhiza esculentus, also known as golden droproot or American goldenseal, is a perennial plant native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can aid in treating infections, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Oncorhiza esculentus include berberine, a key alkaloid responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds such as berbamine and oxyberberine. The most common herbal preparations made with Oncorhiza esculentus include dried root extracts and capsules, as well as topical creams and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
